texto: Rock'n'roll is a teenage sport, meant to be played by teenagers of all ages--they could be 15, 25 or 35. It all boils down to whether they've got the love in their hearts, that beautiful teenage spirit... -Calvin Johnson

texto: For Nirvana, putting out their first major-label record was like getting into a new car. But the runaway success was like suddenly discovering that the car was a Ferrari and the accelerator pedal was Krazy Glued to the floorboard.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Michael Azerrad: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “For Nirvana, putting out their first major-label record was like getting into a new car. But the …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, success, new-car pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de new-car e new.

texto: When you're writing, you're only a brain and some fingers, but drumming, you're involving all four limbs, and you're hearing stuff and you're converting your ideas into physical motions, getting physical feedback from things you are touching - it's pretty cool. It's a really a nice contrast to writing.
